occupation: Chief Executive Officer of Primedia Out of Home and Primedia Studios at Primedia Group
headline: CEO of Primedia Out of Home and Primedia Studios
summary: A highly skilled Chief Executive with more than 19 years experience in media, FMCG, consultancy, and IT services,  specialising in strategic, operations and business management. Bongumusa has specialised in board governance, corporate affairs, business development, business turnarounds, and legal, commercial, and risk management within both private and state owned enterprise. He has a proven track record for optimising business opportunities, improving operational efficiency, and implementing growth initiatives that produce innovative campaigns, enabling clients to achieve their marketing objectives. Bongumusa has been involved in significant merger and acquisition transactions, such as the ABInBev acquisition of SABMiller and the Coca-Cola Beverages Africa agreement.

experiences.title: Chief Executive Officer of Primedia Out of Home and Primedia Studios
experiences.description:
KPI’s: Accountable for the vision and strategic direction, corporate governance, leadership and organisational culture, financial oversight, operational effectiveness, talent development, investor relations, stakeholder engagement, sustainability initiatives, and corporate social responsibility. Have established a solid strategy that ensures business continuity, expansion and reinforces Primedia as major player in the Out-of-Home advertising industry, and Studio productions, leveraging the strengths of both divisions. 

CEO Primedia Outdoor Division: January 2023 to present. 
National and international advertising through roadside billboards, commuter media, mall media, retail and pharmacy outlets, operating in 9 African countries with more than 22,000 advertising sites.

Achievements: Established a successful turnaround strategy and implementation plan.  Achieved a double-digit revenue growth with minimal capex investments.  Achieved a double-digit profit margin growth in both South Africa and Africa.  Improved the staff engagement, morale, and productivity. 
Secured long term lease agreements worth over a billion South African Rand.

CEO Primedia Studios Division: February 2025 to present. 
Media Production Studio with the main focus of  being the “Voice of Africa’s Storytelling”.

Responsible for managing the production studio operations, handling distribution agreements, leading sponsorship initiatives, and strategically positioning content that will share the creative richness of Africa globally.

experiences.title: Chief Executive Officer
experiences.description:
Primedia Outdoor is the premier provider of Out of Home media solutions within South Africa and the Rest of Africa. A Level 1 BBBEE contributor, Primedia Outdoor is currently the largest wholly South-African owned outdoor advertising media specialist offering national outdoor exposure throughout South Africa and high reach in key markets in the Rest of Africa.

Primedia Outdoor delivers exposure opportunities across a mix of outdoor media platforms and audience targeting solutions. Our offering includes high-end digital displays, freeway and suburban spectaculars, street furniture, as well as static advertising.

Primedia Outdoors’ state-of-the-art digital assets are fully equipped with dynamic capabilities which delivers engaging and memorable Outdoor media campaigns harnessed by the latest technology.

experiences.title: CEO and Partner
experiences.description:
Leading the development of the company’s short- and long-term growth strategy.  Provide strategic input and leadership on decision making issues affecting the organization and our portfolio of companies.  Create, improve, implement and enforce policies and procedures of the organization that will improve operational, financial effectiveness and reputation of the company. Creating, communicating, and implementing the organization's vision, mission, and overall direction.
Evaluating the success of the organization in reaching its goals.  Modeling and setting the company’s culture, values, and behavior.

experiences.title: Managing Director
experiences.description:
Established a successful company operating in the public policy and advisory consulting space. Some major Blue-Chip clients: Coca Cola Beverages South Africa (Pty) Ltd (CCBSA) and Distell (Pty) Ltd.

Achievements:  Successfully drove the process of appointing high caliber, competent, executive teams with the prerequisite skills, to assist business turnarounds. Have reduced losses from over ZAR 1 billion for one client. Successfully worked with Government Departments and Parliament in an advisory capacity. Have developed and implemented new policies and procedures to improve clients' corporate governance, market share, and reputation. Have improved revenue growth of clients.
experiences.location: City of Johannesburg, Gauteng, South Africa

experiences.title: Executive Director of Legal and External Affairs
experiences.description: Reported to CEO. I was responsible for providing leadership across the Southern African Market on all matters pertaining to regulatory issues, excise, trade, tobacco control, reputation management, anti-Illicit trade and security. Devised strategies to lead and manage external engagements, mitigating risk and maximising competitive advantages. Evaluated local competitor strategies (including illegal competitors) and lead development of appropriate responses.   Ensured high standards of corporate governance. Provided direction, leadership and talent development. Gave input to the Commercial strategy and five-year corporate plan.  Formulated opinions on a range of topics through primary and secondary research and analysis.   Supported global leadership and achieved organisations revenue targets.  Worked directly with Senior Stakeholders, including Global Leadership teams and Board Members. Worked independently to oversee multiple projects simultaneously and developed business strategies.  Lead the Legal functions strategy development and oversaw the execution. Was responsible for the business licence to trade and advised the board on regulatory and transformation issues.

experiences.title: Executive Director of Corporate Affairs and Transformation
experiences.description: Reported to the CEO. Was responsible for Public Policy, Stakeholder Engagement, Regulatory Affairs, Sustainability, Communication and Reputation management.  Achievements: Succeeded and excelled in Stakeholder engagements and difficult interactions with the Regulator and other government stakeholders. Led and facilitated the development of the Corporate Affairs Strategy and Operational plan. Developed the custom and excise strategy and the implementation plan, which yielded excellent results. Achievements: Delayed the reclassification of Fruit Fermented Beverages saving over ZAR 200 million.  Negotiated partnerships with the Department of Trade and Industry, Department of Transport, Department of Social Development, National Youth Development Agency and the Presidency. These partnerships helped influence perceptions and influenced the regulatory agenda. Negotiated with SARS and the National Treasury to get a new category for grain-based fermented products created specifically for SAB. Negotiated and lobbied for the re-positioning of SAB with the ANC, DA, Traditional leaders and the NGO community and other key players. Negotiated for funding from the Department of Trade & Industry for a project aimed at developing farmers which the DTI and SAB were co-funding.

experiences.title: Senior Manager
experiences.description:
Project Management and People development.  Facilitated strategy sessions, solution designs and business process workshops; Provided support, reviews and motivation for the completion of team deliverables i.e.  current state analysis, requirements gathering, business process design,  productivity analyses, requirements traceability matrices, solution design documents, and design issue resolutions; 
Led the process of power mapping and understanding the power relations within the organisation and drafting a stakeholder management and project acceptance strategy.  Managed the scope of the team’s efforts in line with the project’s statement of work and facilitating any scope changes required; Led the definition of solutions and was involved with other teams’ solution definition activities for the areas where there was a distinct misalignment between important business processes and the packaged solution being implemented; Supervised the compilation of business processes and ensured that the appropriate re-engineering of the old processes occurred. Led teams in the configuration effort and gap identification and design planning effort to ensure successful prototyping of the solutions obtaining unconditional sign-off by all stakeholders approving the future solution and gap closure plans.

education.description: Subjects: strategy; accounting and management; technology, operations and innovation; negotiation, organizations and markets; marketing; and business, government and International economics.

education.activities_and_societies: Activities and Societies: Student Representative Council. Peer Counselling. Student Development. Student Mentorship.
